name: Notion
tagline: Pages, databases, and comments as agent-editable workspace memory.
description: Notion's hosted MCP server lets agents search, create, and reorganize workspace content. The REST API underneath uses internal integrations with explicit page-level sharing — a natural permission boundary for agents.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0
methods.note: The MCP server and public integrations use OAuth.

methods.label: Internal integration secret
methods.note: For personal/workspace automations; pages must be explicitly shared with the integration.

auth.guide:
**MCP with OAuth (easiest):** add `https://mcp.notion.com/mcp` in an OAuth-capable client and approve access.

```bash
claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp
```

**Internal integration (REST):** create one at [notion.so/profile/integrations](https://www.notion.so/profile/integrations) and copy the secret (`ntn_…`). Crucially, **integrations see nothing by default** — open each page or database the agent should access and share it via *⋯ → Connections → your integration*:

```bash
curl https://api.notion.com/v1/search \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NOTION_TOKEN" \
  -H "Notion-Version: 2022-06-28" \
  -X POST
```

The `Notion-Version` header is required on every request. The page-sharing model doubles as your agent's permission scope: share only what it should touch.
